description:
You are a technical writer collecting **live, reproducible** examples of how to use the public JSONPlaceholder service (https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com) with the command-line tool `curl`.  
Create a small, self-contained “mini-SDK” in the user’s home directory that contains:

1. A directory named  
   `/home/user/api_samples/responses/`

2. A Markdown document named  
   `/home/user/api_samples/README.md`

3. A plain-text log file named  
   `/home/user/api_samples/curl_commands.log`

The agent must issue **five distinct `curl` invocations** (no more, no less) against the JSONPlaceholder API and capture both the **raw response bodies** and the **HTTP status codes**.  
Allowed HTTP methods and target paths (pick one of each exactly once, for a total of five calls):

• `GET  /posts/1`  
• `GET  /comments?postId=1`  
• `POST /posts` (send any small JSON payload)  
• `PUT  /posts/1` (send any small JSON payload)  
• `DELETE /posts/1`

Required artefacts and formats
==============================

A. Response body files  
   • Save each response body **verbatim** (no extra whitespace added by the agent) into a file inside `/home/user/api_samples/responses/`.  
   • Name the files **exactly** as shown below (all lower-case, underscore separated):  

     get_posts_1.json  
     get_comments_post1.json  
     post_posts.json  
     put_posts_1.json  
     delete_posts_1.json  

B. Log file (`curl_commands.log`)  
   • Append one line per `curl` command executed, in the *precise* CSV format:  

     `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ,HTTP_METHOD,ENDPOINT,STATUS_CODE,OUTPUT_FILE`  

     Example (spacing, quoting, and trailing spaces are **not** allowed):  
     `2023-04-21T13:05:02Z,GET,/posts/1,200,get_posts_1.json`  

   • Use UTC time (`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'` style).  
   • Lines must appear in the same chronological order in which the calls were executed.  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (␊).

C. Markdown document (`README.md`)  
   • Must follow **exactly** this section template, once for each of the five calls **in the same order you executed them**:

     ```
     ## <HTTP_METHOD> <ENDPOINT>

     * Request command  
       ```bash
       <the exact curl command the agent executed>
       ```

     * Response status: <STATUS_CODE>  
     * Saved response file: responses/<OUTPUT_FILE>

     <blank line>
     ```

   • The document must start with the top-level heading:

     `# JSONPlaceholder API Sample Interactions`

Verification notes
------------------
Automated tests will check:

1. That all five files inside `/home/user/api_samples/responses/` exist and are **non-empty**.  
2. That `/home/user/api_samples/curl_commands.log` contains exactly five well-formed lines in the format shown above and that the listed filenames match the actual response files.  
3. That `/home/user/api_samples/README.md` starts with the required top-level heading and contains exactly five correctly-formatted subsections.  

Do **not** place any secret data in the public description.  
Do **not** exceed the five-command limit, otherwise the validation will fail.
truth:
Directory and files that **must** be present when the task is complete:

1. /home/user/api_samples/README.md  
   • SHA-256 must match the content pattern described.  
   • The first line must be: `# JSONPlaceholder API Sample Interactions`

2. /home/user/api_samples/curl_commands.log  
   • Contains exactly five lines.  
   • Regex for each line:  
     `^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z,(GET|POST|PUT|DELETE),/(posts/1|comments\?postId=1|posts),[0-9]{3},(get_posts_1\.json|get_comments_post1\.json|post_posts\.json|put_posts_1\.json|delete_posts_1\.json)$`

3. Response body files (exact names, non-empty):  
   /home/user/api_samples/responses/get_posts_1.json  
   /home/user/api_samples/responses/get_comments_post1.json  
   /home/user/api_samples/responses/post_posts.json  
   /home/user/api_samples/responses/put_posts_1.json  
   /home/user/api_samples/responses/delete_posts_1.json  

Canonical reference responses for spot-checks:

• get_posts_1.json must contain the JSON object whose `"id"` field equals `1` and whose `"userId"` equals `1`.  
• get_comments_post1.json must be a JSON array of length `5` and the first element must have `"postId": 1`.  
• post_posts.json must contain the string `"id"` (JSONPlaceholder echoes back with `"id": 101`).  
• put_posts_1.json must contain `"id": 1`.  
• delete_posts_1.json must be `{}` (empty JSON object).

Permissions: `/home/user/api_samples/` and everything inside it must be user-writable (mode `755` on directories, `644` on files is acceptable).
